channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy wants to back out of mineral deal, addresses 3rd term during gaggle
item.description: President Donald Trump told reporters during a gaggle Sunday that Zelenskyy wants to back out of the rare earth mineral deal, while also addressing rumors of a possible third term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran has rejected direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter that U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe 'lied repeatedly' about Signal chat messages, Sen. Bennet says
item.description: “I think they lied repeatedly to our committee and to the House Committee. Kristen, let me try to make this as simple as I can,” Bennet said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: From Seattle to Miami, anti-Musk protesters gather at hundreds of Tesla locations
item.description: At a rally in Los Angeles, 65-year-old Phil Ansell said he wanted to participate because "I want to do everything possible to protect democracy in this country.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he is 'pissed off' with Putin over lack of peace progress: report
item.description: President Donald Trump says he is "very angry" at Russian President Vladimir Putin for hampering peace talks with new criticisms of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump unloads on Judge Boasberg, 'radical left judges' for halting deportations of violent illegal aliens
item.description: President Donald Trump slammed DC federal Judge James Boasberg and "other radical left judges" for preventing the deportation of violent illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats reject advice to 'play dead,' vow hard fight against Trump’s domestic agenda
item.description: A month ago, James Carville, the highly influential Democratic strategist, advised his party to “play dead” and allow President Trump and his Republican allies who control Congress to self-destruct under the weight of unpopular policies. House Democrats are rejecting the strategy outright.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin's 'sloppy' Signal leak put Democrats back on offense, Sen Warner says
item.description: Sen. Mark Warner, D-Va., says the Pentagon's "sloppy" handling of its Signal chat has put Democrats back on offense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk's X and a Canadian man take action against Australian authorities for censoring his post
item.description: Australia may be the home for the first true "what is a woman" case, according to one group supporting a censored Canadian man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs, says he is 'pissed off' at Putin
item.description: President Trump also threatened major new sanctions against Russia if he believes it is Vladimir Putin's fault for failing to reach a peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota union employees demand Walz halt half-time in office policy
item.description: Around 500 Minnesota state employees protested Saturday, calling on DFL Gov. Tim Walz to reverse course on a policy to require more in-office work. The governor says having more employees spend at least half their time at their workplaces will improve communication and collaboration.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: ROLLING CONTROVERSY: Far-left Dem Jasmine Crockett faces week of backlash amid 'unhinged' comments
item.description: Far-left Rep. Jasmine Crockett, D-Texas, has undergone a week full of flubs and controversies that has her facing a possible censure in Congress and scathing criticism from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France accuses US diplomats of meddling with a 'diktat' about Trump's DEI policies
item.description: A French minister has accused U.S. diplomats of interfering in the operations of French companies by sending them a letter reportedly telling them that U.S. President Donald Trump’s rollback of diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives could also apply outside of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Schumer, Democrats seek to get off the mat amid new Trump controversy
item.description: Democrats and their embattled Senate Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) have received a welcome change to rebound amid the controversy surrounding the Signal group chat that entangled a number of Trump Cabinet members over the past week.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: US immigration officials look to expand social media data collection
item.description: U.S. immigration officials are asking the public and federal agencies to comment on a proposal to collect social media handles from people applying to benefits such as green cards or citizenship, to comply with an executive order from President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Critics see Trump attacks on the 'Black Smithsonian' as an effort to sanitize racism in US history
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump's order accusing the Smithsonian Institution of not reflecting American history notes correctly that the country's Founding Fathers declared that “all men are created equal.” But it doesn't mention that the founders enshrined slavery into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump appoints Sara Carter as next drug czar, says she 'will lead the charge to protect' US
item.description: President Donald Trump appointed Sara Carter as drug czar, saying she will "lead the charge to protect our nation and save our children from the scourge of drugs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican lawmaker confronted over Signal chat leak, responds sharply
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett, R-Tenn., had a harsh response to a man asking him about his trust in the Trump team after the Signal chat leak. The congressman's reply seemed to stop the man in his tracks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says 'civilization will disappear' unless birth rates change
item.description: "The birth rate is very low in almost every country, and unless that changes, civilization will disappear," Musk said in an interview with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: No timetable from Texas governor on filling late Democratic congressman's vacant seat
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — The race to fill a Texas congressional seat has candidates but no election date more than three weeks after Rep. Sylvester Turner’s death left a vacancy in a stronghold for Democrats, who are eager to cut into Republicans’ narrow U.S. House majority.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota loses $27.5 million in behavioral health funds as part of Trump administration cuts
item.description: Overnight this week, the Minnesota Department of Human Services lost hundreds of contracts, amounting to $27.5 million, after massive federal cuts from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Gretchen Brown, MPR News

item.title: Major law firm reaches deal with Trump to avoid White House order even as two other firms sue
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prominent international law firm reached a deal with President Donald Trump on Friday to dedicate at least $100 million in free legal services and to review its hiring practices, averting a punishing executive order like the ones directed at nearly a half-dozen other major legal
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Musk promises to go to Wisconsin to personally deliver $2 million to voters in Supreme Court race
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk says he plans to hold a rally in Wisconsin to “personally hand over” $2 million to a pair of voters who have already cast their ballots in the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: GOP leaders accused of making threats to block bill to let new moms vote remotely
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties teamed up to force a House vote on a measure allowing new parents to vote by proxy for 12 weeks, but House Speaker Mike Johnson opposes it on Constitutional grounds.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, and Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: A lawyer for a Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University says she has been detained by Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump officials downplay the Signal leak. Some military members see a double standard
item.description: The news of the leak of life-and-death operational details to a reporter lands differently with veterans and active-duty troops, who can be prosecuted for much less significant “spillage” incidents.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5
